text.tspan: CLAIM CHECK: don't put large payloads in messages.
Store them in a blob store; put a reference in the message.
Producer sends 50 MB image as a Kafka/SQS message
u2192 broker max message size exceeded (Kafka default 1MB)
u2192 OR: every consumer in fanout holds 50 MB
u2192 OR: dead-letter queues fill up with huge bodies
u2022 payload >> typical message size (rule of thumb: > 256 KB)
u2022 not every consumer needs the payload (lazy fetch)
u2022 cost of replicated message storage > cost of blob store
u2022 long-lived payloads where blob retention != message retention
u2022 payloads are small (just send them inline)
u2022 every consumer always needs full payload + low latency
u2022 strict atomicity needed (blob and message can diverge)
u2022 Azure Service Bus + Blob Storage (Azure documents this pattern explicitly)
u2022 SQS + S3 (very common; AWS extended client lib does this transparently)
u2022 Kafka + S3 / HDFS (common for media / large events)
u2022 Spring Cloud Stream + S3 binder
1. uploads 50 MB image u2192 blob store (S3, Azure Blob, GCS)
u2192 a few hundred BYTES, not 50 MB
2. DECIDES if it needs the payload (maybe it doesn't)
3. IF needed: fetches blob from store
- cached: nearby fetches share storage CDN cache
blob lifecycle policy deletes after retention period
OR cleanup event after all consumers processed
